column1: Warning:\n\n"Leisure Suit Larry in the Land of the Lounge Lizards" contains some elements of plot which may not be  considered appropriate for some children.
column2: 警告：\n\n《休閒西裝賴瑞在鬼混蜥蜴樂園》裡有些劇情，可能不適合小朋友看到。

column1: How old are you?
column2: 你多大？

column1: To verify you are really %v209, please answer five simple questions.
column2: 為了確認你確實是 %v209，請回答五個簡單問題。

column1:
Sorry.

This game can only be played by adults, or with an adult.

Please find an adult, come back, and try again.
column2:
抱歉。

這個遊戲只能由成年人，或跟成年人一起玩。

請找個大人來，再回來試一次。

column1: Oops. You blew that one!\n\nIf you miss another, you can't possibly be %v209.
column2: 哎呀，你這題答錯啦！\n\n再錯一題，你就不可能是 %v209 了！

column1:
You're a kid!!

Shame on you for trying to deceive a friendly game like this!

Please play again when you're older.
column2:
你根本是小孩！！

虧你想騙過這麼友善的遊戲，也太不害臊了吧！

等你長大一點再回來玩吧。

column1: Correct.
column2: 正確。

column1: Thank you.\n\nAnd now, slip into your leisure suit and prepare to enter the "Land of the Lounge Lizards" with "Leisure Suit Larry!"
column2: 謝謝你。\n\n現在，穿上你的休閒西裝，準備跟著「休閒西裝賴瑞」一起走進「鬼混蜥蜴樂園」！

column1: Warning!\n\nThis game may be hazardous to those with heart conditions!
column2: 警告！\n\n心臟不好的人，這遊戲玩下去可能會出事！

column1: No, you're not!
column2: 不，你不是！

column1: Question #%v208. Correct answer is %v211.
column2: 問題 #%v208。正確答案是 %v211。

column1: %v62 cycles in %v63 seconds
column2: %v62 個循環耗時 %v63 秒

column1: Hey! You're not alive yet.
column2: 嘿，你都還沒出生咧，是要怎麼玩遊戲！

column1: The Sierra adventure team races to your rescue, dumping your worthless, leisure suit- clad hulk into their giant bit bucket, for recycling into future games. Their underground workshop is humming with activity. This is "Where Adventurers Are Made, Not Born!"
column2: Sierra 冒險救援隊火速趕來救你，把你這具穿著白西裝、毫無用處的軀殼，直接丟進他們的巨型回收桶，留著回收利用到未來的遊戲裡。他們的地下工作室這下忙翻了。這裡正是「冒險家不是天生的，是量產出來的！」

column1: Commander Cavin quickly assembles a replacement Larry, while subordinates repair players damaged in other Sierra adventure games.
column2: 卡文司令火速組裝了一個備用賴瑞，手下同時忙著修理其他 Sierra 冒險遊戲裡摔壞的玩家。

column1: With your new body and ego, you are returned to fight again the battles of Lost Wages!\n\n(Now you know what we have to go through every time you press that "Restart" key.)
column2: 用你的新身體和新自尊，你被送回去繼續失樂城的戰鬥！\n\n（現在你知道，每次你按下「重新開始」鍵，我們得經歷什麼了吧。）

column1: Larry, when are you gonna learn to stay out of those dark alleys!!
column2: 驚傳！中年男夜闖暗巷慘遭「關切」，當場領便當。賴瑞啊賴瑞，暗巷這款所在是你能亂鑽的？

column1: Hey guys!! He's screwed up again! Dump this one down the tubes, and get him another body!
column2: 「喂，各位！他又把自己搞死了！把這具沖掉，再給他生一個新的來！」

column1: Since you're dead, why are you talking?? Just hang on for the ride.
column2: 都領便當了還在碎念？歹勢啦，坐乎穩別亂動，好好享受這趟單程之旅。

column1: It appears he wants something -- YOU! Get out of here!
column2: 看樣子他想要的東西只有一個——就是你！還不快滾！

column1: The cabbie snarls a "Where to, buddy?" as he swings the meter flag down.
column2: 運將沒好氣地喊了聲：「去哪，老兄？」順手把跳表打下去。

column1: Lookin' for action, eh? Dis town is full of it. We gotta disco that's usually full of foxes this time of night. Den dere's the casino, next to the all-night weddin' chapel. And we gotta lovely bar, but you've been dere. Me, I like da convenience store next to da disco, but of course, that's just personal preference.
column2: 「想找刺激齁？這款小鎮多的是啦。咱有間迪斯可，這時間通常辣妹滿滿。啊，還有間賭場，就在那間全年無休的婚禮教堂隔壁。咱那間酒吧也不錯，你去過啦。我自己是比較愛迪斯可旁邊那間超商啦，個人口味，你隨意。」

column1: It won't take us long to get back dere, but I'll have to circle 'round da block.
column2: 「回去那邊不會太久啦，不過我得先繞個路。」

column1: Well, here we are, buddy. That'll be $%v200.00.
column2: 「好啦，到囉，老兄。該付 $%v200.00 啦。」

column1: So, wadda we gonna do, buddy? Ya just gonna sit here all night? I gotta earn a livin' too, ya know!
column2: 「所以咧，老兄？你是要在這坐一整晚喔？我也是要顧三餐的耶！」

column1: Why don'cha wait'll we git there, buddy?
column2: 「你就不能等到了再說嗎，老兄？」

column1: Sorry, but the meter's runnin'. Where ja wanna go?
column2: 「抱歉啦，表在跳呢。你到底要去哪？」

column1: Thanks, buddy. Maybe I'll see ya again sometime!
column2: 「謝了，老兄。也許哪天還會見面！」

column1: Waddaya tryin' to do, peahead! You're not gonna stiff me, and get away with it!
column2: 「你在唬爛什麼啦，白痴！想賴帳跑路，門都沒有！」

column1: You wish you hadn't. Mr. America, he ain't. His strongest feature seems to be his odor!
column2: 你會後悔多看他一眼。他絕對稱不上什麼型男，唯一讓人印象深刻的，大概就是他那身味道！

column1: He wonders why you keep looking at him, and flashes a coy little smile back at you in the mirror. Your stomach turns, and not from the ride!
column2: 他被你看得莫名其妙，還在後照鏡裡回你一個嬌羞的微笑。你整個胃都翻了——不是因為暈車。

column1: The driver looks at you and says, "Hey! What's that you got? Wine?" He grabs your bottle of wine, and guzzles it down!
column2: 運將瞄了你一眼：「欸，你拿的是啥？紅酒喔？」說時遲那時快，一把搶過你的酒瓶就往嘴裡灌！

column1: Ok, but it'll cost ja extra!
column2: 「好啦，不過要加錢喔！」

column1: Waddaya mean, 'no money!!' snarls the driver. "Step outside my office, chump!"
column2: 「你說『沒錢』是什麼意思！！」運將暴怒。「給我滾出我的『辦公室』，俗辣！」

column1: Ok. You got it, Mac!
column2: 「好啦，沒問題，老兄！」

column1: Hang on, Larry. He's swerving all over the road!
column2: 坐好啊，賴瑞。運將龍飛鳳舞，車子跟蛇一樣扭！

column1: Whew! You're glad to get out of here.
column2: 呼～總算下車了，你鬆了好大一口氣。

column1: It seems a bridge abutment has brought your game to a sudden stop! Remember, Larry: "Friends don't let cabbies drive drunk!"
column2: 看來一根橋墩讓你的遊戲戛然而止！記住啊賴瑞：「夠麻吉，就不會讓運將酒駕！」

column1: I'm sorry, but it's against my personal philosophy to accept a gratuity!
column2: 「歹勢啦，收小費違反我做人的原則！」

column1: Sheepishly, you apologize to the cabbie for not carrying enough money to pay for the ride.
column2: 你尷尬到不行，跟運將道歉，因為身上錢不夠付車資。

column1: So? Are ya gonna get outta my cab, or not?? Thanks for da business, but I didn't want ta make no career outta this!
column2: 「怎樣？你到底下不下車？謝謝惠顧，不過載你這單我不想幹一輩子！」

column1: So? Are ya gonna pay up, or do I have ta get rough with cha??
column2: 「怎樣？你要付錢，還是要我對你來硬的？？」

column1: It seems to be running a little fast, but then, this is an expensive town!
column2: 感覺跳得有點快，不過嘛，這鎮上什麼都貴桑桑！

column1: You wonder if cab companies have full-time employees to make these floors sticky.
column2: 你納悶計程車行是不是專門請人來把地板弄得黏踢踢。

column1: Even the ceiling's grungy.
column2: 連天花板都髒到有剩。

column1: Ok, buddy, says the cabbie. "But why would cha wanna go there?"
column2: 「好啦，老兄，」運將說。「不過你去那邊幹嘛啦？」

column1: He sure drives fast, doesn't he?
column2: 這運將開得真快，是齁？

column1: Nope. Next time, make up your mind before ya gets in da cab!
column2: 「不行啦。下次先想清楚要去哪再上車！」

column1: You are outside Lefty's bar. Isn't it wonderful what can be done with a little neon?
column2: 你站在老左酒吧外。霓虹燈這東西真是神奇，隨便閃兩下就有夜店的感覺，是吧？

column1: There is a sign on that utility pole.
column2: 電線桿上貼了張告示。

column1: It appears that the hooker gave you a little more than you bargained for!
column2: 看來那個小姐給你的，比你原本想的還多得多！

column1: Considering the looks of this place, you decide that next time you'd be better off to spend a little more money on your computer games!
column2: 看這地方的德性，你決定下次還是把錢省下來買電玩比較划算！

column1: While life may be possible, it is no longer worth living.
column2: 你雖然還沒死，但這條命已經不值得活了。

column1: The windows are too dirty to see through.
column2: 窗戶髒到根本看不透。

column1: Nah. Lefty would probably break both your legs!
column2: 別想了。老左大概會直接打斷你兩條腿！

column1: Nice tuck and roll job, eh?
column2: 這一滾摔得挺專業的嘛，齁？

column1: Welcome
column2: Welcome

column1: Amazed by your own cleverness, you lift the door mat and discover...
column2: 你對自己的機智佩服得五體投地，掀開門墊，結果發現……

column1: ...absolutely nothing!
column2: ……什麼都沒有！

column1: (What did you expect? A key?)
column2: （你以為會有什麼？鑰匙嗎？）

column1: You can't. The door is securely locked.
column2: 不行。門鎖得死死的。

column1: Lefty's closes at 3:00 a.m.
column2: 老左酒吧凌晨 3 點打烊。

column1: STOP, PERVERT!!! shouts the cop.\n\n"I wonder who he's yelling at," you think.
column2: 「站住，色狼！！！」警察大吼。\n\n「不知道他在吼誰咧，」你心想。

column1: Caught you, pervert! the cop shouts. "We don't like people exposing themselves around this town, especially wearing one of those!"
column2: 「抓到你了，色狼！」警察怒喝。「我們這鎮上最看不慣有人隨便曝光，尤其還穿著那種東西！」

column1: Next time, if you insist on wearing your "lubber" in public, you should at least zip your fly.
column2: 下次你堅持要在外面穿那件「橡皮裝」，好歹拉個拉鍊吧。

column1: He appears to be chasing someone!
column2: 他好像在追什麼人！

column1: Try moving to the east end of the fire escape.
column2: 試著移動到逃生梯的東邊。

column1: Try moving to the west end of the fire escape.
column2: 試著移動到逃生梯的西邊。

column1: %m7 and a fence.
column2: %m7 和一道柵欄。

column1: Nah. That room needs the fresh air!
column2: 算了吧。那房間正需要透透氣！

column1: You are on a fire escape. A rusty ladder is at one end. East of the fire escape is another window, just out of your reach.
column2: 你站在逃生梯上。一端有座生鏽的梯子。逃生梯東邊還有一扇窗，可惜就是搆不到。

column1: %m7, a fence, and something sitting in the east window.
column2: %m7、一道柵欄，還有東邊窗裡坐著的東西。

column1: It's a nice little alley, with a trash bin, two windows, a fire escape
column2: 這是條還算像樣的小巷，有垃圾桶、兩扇窗、一座逃生梯

column1: Move closer to the ladder.
column2: 靠近梯子一點。

column1: (Of course, you can't play the violin now, but you get the idea.)
column2: （當然啦，你現在拉不了小提琴，不過意思你懂。）

column1: Unlock it? From the outside?
column2: 開鎖？從外面開？

column1: You can't. It's locked from the inside.
column2: 不行。這鎖是從裡面反鎖的。

column1: Looking inside the west window, you see the room you just left.
column2: 你往西邊窗戶裡看，看到的正是你剛離開的房間。

column1: Looking at the east window, you notice a bottle of pills sitting on the window ledge.
column2: 你看向東邊窗戶，注意到窗台上放著一瓶藥丸。

column1: There are two windows high above you. The west window by the fire escape has a dim, reddish glow; the east window is dark. There may be something on the sill of the east window, but you can't make it out from down here.
column2: 頭頂高處有兩扇窗。逃生梯旁的西邊窗透著暗紅微光；東邊窗一片漆黑。東邊窗台上似乎有東西，但你在下面看不清楚。

column1: You broke it, didn't you?
column2: 你把它弄壞了，對吧？

column1: You lean far out over the railing before remembering that agility is yet another of your short suits.
column2: 你整個人探出欄杆外，這才想起「靈活」從來不是你的強項。

column1: With the rope securely holding you to the railing, you can reach all the way over to the window. NOW, of all times, you notice the window's locked!
column2: 繩子把你牢牢綁在欄杆上，你總算搆到窗戶了。偏偏這時候，你才發現窗子鎖著！

column1: Subtly, you smash the window with your hammer. Your future as a second-story man seems quite plausible!
column2: 你「低調」地掄起榔頭砸破窗戶。看來你這輩子改行當夜賊，前途無量！

column1: You got 'em! But what are they?
column2: 你拿到了！可是……這是啥？

column1: You might try reaching over to the window first.
column2: 你可以先試試把手伸向窗戶。

column1: You consider sticking your hand through a solid glass window, but realize that if you did, you wouldn't be able to play the violin.
column2: 你考慮把手直接穿過整片玻璃窗，但轉念一想，那樣以後就拉不了小提琴了。

column1: It already is.
column2: 它已經是了。

column1: What is this; a death wish?
column2: 這是怎樣，你是活得不耐煩了？

column1: You already took them. Why do this again?
column2: 你已經拿過了。幹嘛又來一次？

column1: Amidst assorted, odoriferous refuse from Lefty's clientel, you discover Lefty's old, left-handed hammer.
column2: 在老左客人們留下的各色餿味垃圾中，你挖出了老左那把老舊的左手榔頭。

column1: There's nothing else here worth describing, let alone taking.
column2: 這裡沒別的東西值得多說，更別提拿走了。

column1: YUCK!!
Unfortunately, you can't see much from out here.
column2: 可惜，你在外面看不出個所以然。

column1: Wow! Does your head hurt!!
column2: 哇賽！你的頭超痛的啦！！

column1: Hey! Where are you?
column2: 喂！你在哪？

column1: After a careful analysis of your surroundings, you conclude you are at the bottom of a pile of garbage!
column2: 你仔細分析一番周遭環境，得出結論：你正躺在一堆垃圾的最底層！

column1: Move to the front of the trash bin.
column2: 移動到垃圾桶前面。

column1: There's no way to reach the ladder from down here. It's only accessible from above.
column2: 從下面根本碰不到梯子，只能從上面過去。

column1: You see alleys stretching off to infinity.
column2: 你看見小巷一路延伸到天邊。

column1: The fence is much too tall for you to climb over.
column2: 這柵欄高到你根本爬不過去。

column1: It's not long enough to reach from here.
column2: 從這裡長度不夠，搆不到。

column1: It's not long enough to reach anywhere, but you might want to use it for safety purposes.
column2: 它的長度根本搆不到任何地方，不過留著保命倒是可以。

column1: Be more specific.
column2: 講清楚一點啦。

column1: You untie the rope.
column2: 你解開了繩子。

column1: Using the dull pocket knife, you cut the rope free.
column2: 你拿那把鈍到不行的小刀，把繩子割斷了。

column1: A rickety fire escape, complete with safety ladder, is loosely attached to the building. A mild, reddish glow comes from the window behind it.
column2: 一座搖搖欲墜的逃生梯，附帶安全梯，鬆鬆垮垮地掛在大樓上。後方窗戶透出淡淡的紅光。

column1: Unfortunately the lid on the trash bin just will not close.
column2: 可惜垃圾桶蓋就是關不起來。

column1: Try as you might, it is impossible to move the trash bin.
column2: 你怎麼使勁，垃圾桶都紋風不動。

column1: The solution is closer to you than that.
column2: 答案其實近在眼前，沒你想的那麼遠。

column1: There is an old neon sign hanging on the wall that says "Hotel"...
column2: 牆上掛著一塊舊霓虹招牌，寫著「Hotel」……

column1: ...approximately.
column2: ……大概啦。

column1: You can't. Even its rusty bolts are too tough for you!
column2: 不行。就連那生鏽的螺栓都硬得你搞不定！

column1: Not now!
column2: 現在不行！

column1: There may be something in the east window, but from here, you're not sure.
column2: 東邊窗戶裡好像有東西，不過你在這看不準。

column1: It's a long way to the ground!
column2: 離地面這麼遠，摔下去可不是開玩笑的！

column1: Move closer to the window, if you want to go back in the hooker's room.
column2: 想回那位小姐的房間，就靠近窗戶一點。

column1: You reconsider after noticing the trash bin is behind where you would fall!
column2: 你注意到摔下去的地方剛好是垃圾桶，於是打消了念頭！

column1: You are in Lefty's one and only restroom. The stench is overpowering, there is graffiti on the walls, and you doubt that the sink was clean even when it was installed. Even roaches can't survive in this place!
column2: 你在老左酒吧唯一的一間廁所裡。臭到快讓人斷氣，牆上塗鴉一堆，你懷疑這洗手台裝上去那天就沒乾淨過。連蟑螂都活不下去！

column1: %m8Scott me up, Beamie!"
column2: %m8Scott me up, Beamie!"

column1: Ahhhhhhh.
column2: 啊——嗯，爽。

column1: %m8Attention arcade game players: please don't eat the urinal cakes!"
column2: %m8Attention arcade game players: please don't eat the urinal cakes!"

column1: %m8It takes leather balls to play rugby!"
column2: %m8It takes leather balls to play rugby!"

column1: Funny. No one knocks back.
column2: 好笑，沒人敲回來。

column1: NOW??
column2: 現在？？

column1: You see many messages. One is:\n\n"
column2: 你看到牆上一堆留言，其中一則寫著：\n\n"

column1: You see a handsome, dashing, macho, sexy, young man. (Obviously, this mirror has quite a bit of distortion!)
column2: 你看到一位英俊瀟灑、陽剛性感的年輕帥哥。（很明顯，這面鏡子扭曲得不輕！）

column1: You grab an ancient copy of the "Ball Street Journal," and settle in to "sit and think!"
column2: 你抓起一本古董級的《葷經日報》，準備坐下來「好好沉思」！

column1: You feel a great relief!
column2: 你感到無比的解脫！

column1: Why, look! Some woman removed her diamond ring to wash her hands, and forgot to take it with her.
column2: 咦，你看！有個女人洗手時脫下鑽戒，結果忘記帶走了。

column1: Got it!\n\nYou briefly consider an attempt to find the ring's owner, but return to your normal self and pocket it instead.
column2: 到手了！\n\n你一度想找失主歸還，但很快恢復本性，順手塞進口袋。

column1: A small, neatly lettered sign reminds you:\n\n"Since we can't clean up after every customer, please rinse out this sink when you are finished."
column2: 一塊字跡工整的小告示提醒你：\n\n「本店無法逐一為客人清理，用畢請自行沖洗水槽。」

column1: Evidently, you are the first customer who can read.
column2: 看樣子，你是第一個識字的客人。

column1: My, my. Aren't we the little sick-o!
column2: 哎唷喂，我們還真是個小變態呢！

column1: Say, here's an interesting one:\n\n"The password is: 'Ken sent me.'"
column2: 欸，這則挺有意思：\n\n「暗號是：『肯叫我來的』」

column1: Bad idea, eh Larry?
column2: 爛主意吧，賴瑞？

column1: Your life passes before your eyes.
column2: 你的一生在眼前快轉而過。

column1: You doze briefly.
column2: 你打了個小盹。

column1: What's that aroma?
column2: 那是什麼味道？

column1: It's not a pretty sight!
column2: 那畫面真不忍卒睹！

column1: Say, there's no soap.
column2: 欸，沒肥皂啦。

column1: There's no towels.
column2: 沒有毛巾啦。

column1: Move closer to the handle.
column2: 靠近把手一點。

column1: Your hands are still dirty, but now they're wet!
column2: 手還是髒，不過現在濕答答的！

column1: The toilet paper is only there for official purposes.
column2: 衛生紙純粹是正式用途，別亂想。

column1: Carefully finishing your duties, you wipe and toss the newspaper behind the toilet for the next user.
column2: 小心翼翼完成「大事」後，你擦一擦，把報紙丟到馬桶後面，留給下一位。

column1: You only read while "enthroned."
column2: 你只有「登基」時才看報。

column1: Your face is still dirty, but at least it's now dripping with water.
column2: 臉還是髒，至少現在濕淋淋的了。

column1: Ok. You're dry (but your keyboard is still damp)!
column2: 好了，你乾了（不過你的鍵盤還濕濕的）！

column1: Realizing your mistake, you quickly jiggle the handle, attempting to stem the onrushing tide of water.
column2: 你驚覺搞砸了，趕緊猛搖把手，想擋住那波洶湧而來的水。

column1: It doesn't work.
column2: 沒用啦。

column1: It's nice of you to try, but this sink is too far gone to save!
column2: 你有心是不錯，但這洗手台已經沒救了！

column1: No one would drink from ANY fixture in here!
column2: 這裡任何一個水龍頭，沒人敢喝！

column1: Nice idea, but the mirror is firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 想法不錯，但鏡子牢牢釘死在牆上。

column1: Yuck!
column2: 嘔！

column1: Lefty must be running low on air freshener.
column2: 老左的空氣芳香劑大概快見底了。

column1: No wonder your eyesight is so bad!
column2: 難怪你的視力這麼爛！

column1: Been drinking Lefty's liquor again, eh?
column2: 又在偷灌老左的酒了齁？

column1: You are in a dimly lit hallway. The paint peeling off the walls gives the cockroaches something to watch. An old table is pushed against the west wall. A filthy drunk sits on the filthy floor, leaning against the filthy wall.
column2: 你在一條昏暗的走廊裡。牆上剝落的油漆給蟑螂提供了免費電影。一張舊桌子靠著西牆。一個髒兮兮的醉漢，坐在髒兮兮的地板上，靠著髒兮兮的牆。

column1: Jeeezz! Somethin' die in there??
column2: 「阿娘喂！裡面死了什麼東西啊？？」

column1: You can remember when that table held a rose.
column2: 你還記得，這張桌子曾經擺過一朵玫瑰。

column1: You find nothing but odor.
column2: 你除了臭味，什麼都沒找到。

column1: Hiya shhhhonny. How's 'bout you 'n' me havin' a lil' drink?
column2: 「嘿呀～俊小伙。要不要偶們倆喝一杯啊？」

column1: Glug, glug, glug, glug, glug.
column2: 咕嚕、咕嚕、咕嚕、咕嚕、咕嚕。

column1: Slurp! "Ahhhh! That did hit the ol' spot!"
column2: 咻嚕！「啊～有夠爽的啦！」

column1:
Ya know, ya mus' be ma only frien' in the whole world! Shhoooo, I'm gonna give ya my only posshhess... hhic!	「你造嗎，你一定是偶在這世界上唯一的朋友！所以，偶要把偶唯一的家當送你……呃！
He's a poor little lamb, who has lost his way...	他是隻迷了路的可憐小羔羊……
...and also his bladder control!	……順便也弄丟了膀胱的控制權！
There is a single, lovely, long-stemmed, red rose lying incongruously on the table.	桌上突兀地擺著一朵孤伶伶的長梗紅玫瑰。
Wadda ya tryin' to do; make me a soprano??"
column2: 「你想幹嘛，把偶變女高音喔？？」

column1: Geeze! Them pointy-toed shoes hurt!
column2: 「靠夭！這雙尖頭鞋夾腳夾得要命！」

column1: You generously hand him a dollar.
column2: 你大方地塞了一塊錢給他。

column1: Shhaay, thanksh buddy! How 'bout sshhom'ore?
column2: 「嘿嘿，謝啦老兄！要不要再來一點啊？」

column1: He responds, "Ya know, whad I really need is a good, sshhtiff belt!"
column2: 他回你：「你知影嘸，偶現在最需要的，是一條夠力的皮帶！」

column1: You spend hours carefully examining every brick. You find nothing.
column2: 你花了好幾個鐘頭仔細檢查每一塊磚。屁都沒找到。

column1: You have no drink to give him.
column2: 你身上沒酒可以給他。

column1: There's no one selling anything here. Try the bartender out front.
column2: 這裡沒人在賣東西啦，去問前面的酒保。

column1: He's too drunk to want anything.
column2: 他醉到啥都不想要了。

column1: He's beyond help, but thirsty.
column2: 沒救了，但還很渴。

column1:
...my only posshhess... hhic!	「…我唯一的家私…呃嗝！
...all I got in the world.\n\nWhat'sha guy like me need witta TV remote control!?"
column2: 「…我這輩子的全部家當。\n\n像我這種人要電視遙控器是要幹嘛啦！？」

column1: Hey! Wake up!
column2: 「嘿！醒醒！」

column1: Caught ya! Now open the door, then get your nose out of that manual and start playing this game the right way!
column2: 抓到你了！趕快開門，把鼻子從說明書裡拔出來，正經玩這遊戲啦！

column1: This is the sleaziest bar you've ever been in. And you've been in quite a few.
column2: 這是你這輩子去過最下流的酒吧，而你去過的下流酒吧可不少。

column1: You are standing.
column2: 你站著。

column1: You are sitting.
column2: 你坐著。

column1: You insert a dollar into the juke box, and select a suitably sleazy song.
column2: 你投一塊錢進點唱機，選了首夠下流的歌。

column1: You rap loudly on the naugahyde door. You wonder how many naugas had to give their all just to decorate this sleazehole.
column2: 你大力敲著人造皮門，心想不知道犧牲了多少隻「納霸」才佈置得出這間爛酒吧。

column1: So what?
column2: 所以咧？

column1: Yeah. Whatsda passwoid?
column2: 「是喔，通關密語咧？」

column1: Scram, dog breath!
column2: 「滾啦，你這張狗臭嘴！」

column1: Come on in!
column2: 「進來啦！」

column1: ...blah, blah, blah, blah, blah...
column2: 「…blah blah blah blah…」

column1: ...a lotta guys just leave her hanging in a tree somewhere!
column2: 「…很多人乾脆把她掛樹上就不管了！」

column1: ...'cause Thursday's your night in the barrel!
column2: 「…因為禮拜四就是你倒楣挨桶子的份！」

column1: ...just think of the money he saved!
column2: 「…想想他省下多少錢！」

column1: ...and there stood the pig and the cow!
column2: 「…豬跟牛就這樣站在那裡！」

column1: ...dammit Liza, doncha know ya own reflection?
column2: 「…該死的麗莎，你連自己的倒影都不認得啊？」

column1: ...we are coming back this way, aren't we?
column2: 「…我們等一下還會走這條路吧？」

column1: ...You idiot! I said POSSE!!
column2: 「…你這呆瓜！我說的是『民團』啦！！」

column1: ...nobody can eat THAT much ice cream!
column2: 「…沒有人吃得下那麼多冰淇淋啦！」

column1: ...25 bucks, same as in town!
column2: 「…25 塊，跟鎮上同一個價！」

column1: You'd better sit down first.
column2: 你最好先坐下來。

column1: You greet the bartender.\n\n"What'll it be," Lefty responds, "whiskey, wine or beer?"
column2: 你跟酒保打招呼。\n\n「要喝啥，」老左應道，「威士忌、紅酒還是啤酒？」

column1: I'll have a glass of your fine, well whiskey! you tell Lefty.
column2: 「給我來杯你們家上好的散裝威士忌！」你跟老左說。

column1: Gimme a mug o' beer! you order Lefty.
column2: 「給我一杯啤酒！」你跟老左點餐。

column1: That'll be $3.00, please.
column2: 「三塊，麻煩了。」

column1: You flip three bucks onto the counter.
column2: 你把三塊錢拍上吧檯。

column1: I'm presently a little short, you stammer.
column2: 「我…我現在手頭有點緊，」你結巴地說。

column1: Yeah, and not just in the financial department, I bet! he replies, grabbing back your order.
column2: 「是啊，我看緊的不只是荷包吧！」他邊說邊把你的酒搶回去。

column1: You down the beer in your pseudo-macho style, and slam the mug back on the bar.
column2: 你用你那假掰硬漢的姿勢一口乾了啤酒，把杯子重重砸回吧檯。

column1: You delicately sip the wine until it's all gone.\n\nYou tell Lefty, "I find this impudent and sassy, with the slightest hint of impertinence."
column2: 你優雅地一小口一小口把酒喝光。\n\n你跟老左說：「這酒帶點放肆，又有點調皮，還隱隱透著一絲狂妄。」

column1: He gazes at you longingly and moistens his lips!
column2: 他用渴望的眼神看著你，還舔了舔嘴唇！

column1: Suddenly you feel a little woozy.
column2: 忽然你覺得有點頭重腳輕。

column1: This one tastes even better than the last. One more sounds like a good idea.
column2: 這杯比上一杯還讚。再來一杯感覺是個好主意。

column1: Try sitting at the bar; that usually gets Lefty's attention.
column2: 試試坐在吧檯邊，這樣通常能引起老左注意。

column1: It's not good etiquette to drink standing up.
column2: 站著喝酒不太合禮數。

column1: Since you can only balance one open glass of whiskey at a time, you toss this one down immediately.
column2: 既然你一次只能端一杯開口的威士忌，乾脆直接一口乾了。

column1: May I please have a glass of your delicate white zinfandel, sir? you ask Lefty.
column2: 「可以請您給我一杯溫潤的白金芬黛嗎，先生？」你問老左。

column1: Hey, was that your voice?
column2: 嘿，那是你的聲音嗎？

column1: You don't drink the whiskey, but instead, decide to carry it with you wherever you go, precariously balanced in an open shot glass.
column2: 你沒把威士忌喝掉，反而決定端著它到處走，搖搖晃晃地平衡在那只開口的小杯裡。

column1: The whiskey burns its way down your throat. You vow to never again buy a "well" brand.
column2: 威士忌燒得你喉嚨一路發燙。你發誓再也不買散裝的了。

column1: Hold on; you haven't got your last order yet!
column2: 等一下，你上一杯都還沒拿到咧！

column1: You feel a little light-headed.
column2: 你覺得有點暈頭轉向。

column1: Shaking your head, you clear away the cobwebs and are able to once again walk in your standard, studly style.
column2: 你搖搖頭甩開暈眩，總算又能用你那招牌帥氣步伐走路了。

column1: Cram it, clown! you tell the bore on the stool next to you!
column2: 「閉嘴啦，小丑！」你對隔壁高腳椅上的無聊傢伙說！

column1: Same to ya! shouts Lefty.
column2: 「彼此彼此啦！」老左喊道。

column1: It's an antique, left over from "King's Quest III."
column2: 這是古董，是《King's Quest III》留下來的。

column1: Patience, Larry; patience!
column2: 耐心點，賴瑞，耐心點！

column1: The door is locked. And, there is no key.
column2: 門鎖住了，而且沒有鑰匙。

column1: She may not be a great looker, but think of the muscles in that leg.
column2: 她長相不怎樣，但你看看那條腿上的肌肉。

column1: It's just going around in circles...
column2: 只是不斷繞圈子…

column1: ...like your life.
column2: …就跟你的人生一樣。

column1: You don't know much about art, but you do know what you like!
column2: 你不太懂藝術，但你知道自己喜歡啥！

column1: You mean to say you're playing a 3-D adventure game, and all you want is LIGHT beer??
column2: 你是說你在玩 3D 冒險遊戲，結果只想喝淡啤酒？？

column1: They're just sitting at the bar, drinking their livers into oblivion.
column2: 他們就坐在吧檯，把肝喝到報廢。

column1: Sorry, we don't sell them sissy drinks here, says Lefty. "We got whiskey, wine and beer. What do you want?"
column2: 「歹勢，我們這不賣那種娘泡飲料，」老左說。「只有威士忌、紅酒跟啤酒，你要啥？」

column1: My, aren't we the little savage! Didn't your mother teach you anything?
column2: 唉呦，你這野蠻小子！你媽都沒教過你嗎？

column1: Who cares, she replies sarcastically. "Stop bugging me, or I'll have my boy friend take care of you when he gets back from the rest room!"
column2: 「誰在乎啊，」她酸溜溜地回。「別煩我，不然等我男友上完廁所回來，他會好好『招待』你！」

column1: Looks like you're operating at your standard level of charm, Larry.
column2: 看來你又發揮了一貫水準的魅力嘛，賴瑞。

column1: Har, har!!
He has nothing to say, but says it continuously.
column2: 他啥都沒說，但一直不停地說。

column1: Hi there, lovely legs! you say sweetly. "My name's Larry; Larry Laffer."
column2: 「嗨，美腿！」你甜言蜜語地說。「我叫賴瑞，賴瑞・賴福。」

column1: They have no comment.
column2: 他們沒意見。

column1: He's not bothering you now; leave him alone, you big bully!
column2: 他現在又沒煩你，放他一馬啦，你這大惡霸！

column1: Gee, Dad. It's a Wurlitzer!
column2: 哇塞，老爸，這是台 Wurlitzer 唱片機耶！

column1: The peephole is locked from the inside.
column2: 窺視孔從裡面鎖住了。

column1: Boy, are you drunk!
column2: 天啊，你醉茫茫了！

column1: SLAP!
column2: 啪！

column1: Ya got me!
column2: 「你抓到我啦！」

column1: No, thanks, Lefty replies. "I don't drink."
column2: 「不用了，」老左說。「我不喝酒。」

column1: Ok, says Lefty, "but that'll cost ya $%v90.00!"
column2: 「好啦，」老左說，「不過要花你 $%v90.00 喔！」

column1: The last of the big time spenders, you go down in a blaze of glory, ordering drinks for everybody in the place.

Press F8 to stand.

To change the amount of your bet, type "change bet."
column2:
準備好按 F4 開始。

要牌按 F6。

停牌按 F8。

改下注金額輸入「change bet」。

column1: Stop staring there, she says softly.

column1: Really? I'm not that kind of girl.
column2: 「真的假的?我才不是那種女孩。」

column1: Evidently the rooms on this floor are all unoccupied.
column2: 看來這層樓的房間都是空的。

column1: Her eyes are as sensuous as her lips.
column2: 她的眼神和嘴唇一樣性感撩人。

column1: You're a man of the world, Larry. Perhaps some sort of medical stimulant would help.
column2: 你可是見過世面的男人,賴瑞。也許來點「醫療用」提神劑會有幫助。

column1: She waves back.
column2: 她揮手回應。

column1: The glass elevator only goes down from here. How about you?
column2: 玻璃電梯從這裡只能往下,你呢?

column1: I'm sorry, but I can't.
column2: 「抱歉,我沒辦法。」

column1: There's no need to do that.
column2: 沒必要這麼做。

column1: Usually when I'm with someone romantic! she replies.
column2: 「通常是跟浪漫的人在一起的時候!」她回答。

column1: You know, I'm supposed to be working!
column2: 「你知道嗎,我現在應該在上班!」

column1: Her hair is thick and beautiful, falling in cascades around her delicate shoulders.
column2: 她濃密秀麗的長髮,如瀑布般披散在纖細的肩上。

column1: Her lips are moist and inviting, but then, you also felt that way about old Mr. Weissenborn, your high school shop teacher!
column2: 她的雙唇濕潤誘人——不過說真的,你當年看高中木工老師 Weissenborn 先生時,好像也有過同樣的感覺!

column1: I'm sorry," Faith replies, "but you don't know me that well!"
column2: 「抱歉,」費絲回答,「但你根本沒那麼了解我!」

column1: Well, now. Isn't THAT special!
column2: 「哎呦,這下可真是特別呢!」

column1: Perhaps, she says, "but right now, I'm working."
column2: 「也許吧,」她說,「不過我現在在上班。」

column1: I'll be here all night.
column2: 「我整晚都會在這。」

column1: The moon lights an expansive rooftop garden with a spa in one corner. You've found the source of the gurgling noise!
column2: 月光灑在寬闊的屋頂花園上,角落有座溫泉池。你終於找到那咕嚕咕嚕聲的源頭了!

column1: Wasting no time, you hop into the water. Oooh, does it feel good! The warm water swirls about you, the bubbles stimulating you nearly as much as the beautiful woman across from you. With the warmth of the water soaking into your body, you become quite relaxed.
column2: 你毫不猶豫跳進水裡。喔,感覺真是太棒了!溫水在你身邊打轉,氣泡帶來的刺激感,幾乎不輸給對面那位美女。溫暖的水浸透全身,你整個人都放鬆了下來。

column1: Wow! Just the way you like your women: nearby and naked! She has a beautiful tan, and a gorgeous body. You've waited a lifetime for this moment.
column2: 哇!正是你喜歡的那款女人:近在眼前又一絲不掛!她有著漂亮的古銅色肌膚和火辣身材。你這輩子就是在等這一刻。

column1: What a beautiful face! She's leaning back, relaxing in the spa with the water bubbling up around her. It's even possible you could become sexually aroused by this!
column2: 好一張美麗的臉蛋!她仰躺在溫泉裡放鬆,水泡在她身邊咕嚕作響。這下,你搞不好真的會有點「性」致勃勃喔!

column1: Eve takes your apple, and with a sweet, innocent look, raises it to her mouth. She takes a small bite out of it, while a sexy smile comes across her face. She winks, and lies back in the water, chewing delicately.
column2: 伊芙接過你的蘋果,帶著甜美無邪的表情舉到嘴邊。她輕輕咬了一小口,臉上浮現一抹性感的微笑。她眨了眨眼,躺回水裡,細細地咀嚼著。

column1: Eve gently moves her sensuous tongue around the apple, tiny drops of the apple's juice glistening on her lips.
column2: 伊芙用性感的舌尖輕輕舔繞著蘋果,晶瑩的果汁在她唇邊閃閃發亮。

column1: In the hot tub is the most beautiful girl you've ever seen! Beside her is her towel, and swim suit. You can't believe she doesn't mind your presence here.
column2: 溫泉池裡是你這輩子見過最美的女孩!她身旁放著毛巾和泳衣。你簡直不敢相信她一點都不介意你在這裡。

column1: You have no apple.
column2: 你沒有蘋果。

column1: Looking over at the next hotel, you notice an ugly girl getting undressed.
column2: 你往隔壁旅館一瞥,發現一個長相普通的女孩正在寬衣解帶。

column1: She's not really that pretty.
column2: 她其實沒有很漂亮。

column1: Well, she's looking better.
column2: 嗯,她看起來順眼多了。

column1: You know, she's not that bad.
column2: 其實,她也沒那麼糟嘛。

column1: You could ignore some of her faults.
column2: 有些缺點好像也可以睜一隻眼閉一隻眼。

column1: Perhaps you're in love!
column2: 搞不好,你戀愛了!

column1: Pant, pant, pant, pant, pant.
column2: 喘、喘、喘、喘、喘。

column1: Something tells you that's the wrong approach with this one, Larry.
column2: 直覺告訴你,這招對這位小姐行不通,賴瑞。

column1: She's really turning you on! You had no idea fruit could be so exciting.
column2: 她真的把你撩到不行!你從沒想過水果也能這麼刺激。

column1: Bad breath's one thing, Mister; but you could knock a buzzard off a manure truck!
column2: 「口臭是一回事,先生;但你這口臭簡直能把禿鷹從糞車上薰下來!」

column1: Nah. Who wants to go hot-tubbing alone?
column2: 才不要,誰想自己一個人泡溫泉啊?

column1: Hey, handsome, why don't you slip into the water with me, so we can REALLY get to know each other!
column2: 「嘿,帥哥,不如你也下水陪我,我們才能『好好』認識彼此!」

column1: Her hand is resting near a fluffy, white, expensive bath towel, embroidered in florid script with the name: "Eve."
column2: 她的手擱在一條蓬鬆的白色高級浴巾旁,上面用花體字繡著名字:「伊芙」。

column1: You can take a hint. "Let's go, baby!" you whisper!
column2: 你看得懂暗示。「走吧,寶貝!」你低聲說!

column1: This water feels SO good!
column2: 「這水感覺真的超讚!」

column1: After finishing the apple, Eve slowly slides out of the hot tub, her steamy, naked body glistening in the moonlight. Barely covering her tremendous assets with the towel, she smiles and motions for you to follow her.
column2: 吃完蘋果後,伊芙緩緩從溫泉裡起身,她蒸騰的裸體在月光下閃閃發亮。用毛巾勉強遮住她傲人的身材,她微笑著示意你跟上。

column1: Who loves ya, baby?

column1: So long, baby! you shout!
column2: 「掰啦,寶貝!」你大喊!

This has to be the kinkiest thing you've done in a long time, Larry.\n\nYou gradually increase your tempo as you lose your inhibitions (not to mention your self-esteem). column1: KEYBOARD COMMANDS
column2: 鍵盤操作

column1: Use arrows or number keys to move
column2: 用方向鍵或數字鍵移動

column1: Larry.
column2: Larry。

column1: Press ESC to use the menus.
column2: 按 ESC 開啟選單。

column1: Press ENTER to choose from a menu,
column2: 按 ENTER 選取選單項目，

column1: or ESC to return to the game.
column2: 或按 ESC 回到遊戲。

column1: Some menu choices list a function key
column2: 有些選單項目會列出功能鍵

column1: shortcut beside their name.
column2: 「快捷鍵」，就標在名稱旁邊。

column1: For a Hint Book, call (209) 683-6858.
(For a good time, call the author!)
column2: （想找點刺激？打電話給作者本人吧！）

column2: 搖桿操作

column1: Point joystick in desired direction
column2: 把搖桿指向你想去的方向

column2: 按上方按鈕開啟選單。

column1: Press other button to see the status
column2: 按另一顆按鈕查看狀態

column1: screen.
column2: 畫面。

column2: 滑鼠操作

column2: 把游標移到你想讓賴瑞

column1: go, then click the button.
column2: 去的地方，然後按一下按鈕。

column1: Press right button to use menus.
column2: 按右鍵開啟選單。

column1: Press left button in the window
column2: 在視窗標題列

column1: bar to use menus.
column2: 按左鍵開啟選單。

column1: Press any key to return to game.
column2: 按任意鍵回到遊戲。

column1: go, then click left button.
column2: 去，然後按一下左鍵。

column1: Bet is $%v226.00         You have $%v90.00
Bet is $%v225.00         You have $%v90.00
Bet is $%v255.00         You have $%v90.00
Congratulations, you've broken the bank. This casino has a limit of $250.00 per person, and you've reached it. No more for you, Larry!
column2: 恭喜，你把莊家壓垮了！\n這家賭場每人限贏 $250.00，\n你已經封頂啦。沒你的份了，賴瑞！

column1: Dealer=%v222   Player=%v223   Split=%v224
You know, the traffic here is really a killer! You should forget about crossing these streets!
column2: 老實說，這裡的車流根本要人命！\n過馬路這種事你還是死了心吧！

column1: YO!!\n\nTAXI!!!
column2: 「喂！！\n\n計程車！！！」

column1: Vowing to hold your breath forever, you enter this miserable excuse for public transportation.
column2: 發誓要憋氣憋到天荒地老，\n你踏進這台丟人現眼的大眾運輸工具。

column1: It's not very clean.
column2: 一點也不乾淨。

column1: It's not very new.
column2: 一點也不新。

column1: (And those are its good points!)
column2: （這些還算它的優點呢！）

column1: Look out! Here he comes again!
column2: 小心！牠又來了！

column1: Obviously, he thinks you're a fire plug.
column2: 顯然牠把你當成消防栓了。

column1: Dogs like that make you want to keep moving.
column2: 遇到這種狗，你只想拔腿快走。

column1: I can't wait all day, bub!
column2: 「老兄，我可沒空等你一整天！」

column1: You'll not ride with me again!
column2: 「你別想再搭我的車了！」

column1: Sick-o!
column2: 噁心鬼！

column1: After all he's done for you?
column2: 他都為你做了那麼多，你還這樣？

column1: He presently smells quite bad, although still better than you.
column2: 牠現在臭得要命，\n不過還是比你香一點。

column1: TAXI\nSTAND
column2: 「計程車\n招呼站」

column1: It's dark. Real dark.
column2: 黑漆漆的，真的黑到不行。

column1: You'd better get out of the street first.
column2: 你最好先離開馬路。

column1: BEEP\nBEEP!
Thousands of cars stream past, just below the edge of your screen.
column2: 上千輛車呼嘯而過，\n就在你螢幕邊緣底下。

column1: Trust me, they're there!
column2: 相信我，它們真的在那裡！

column1: It smells like an old cab.
column2: 聞起來像台老計程車。

column1: Isn't it funny how there's so little traffic?
column2: 說也奇怪，怎麼車這麼少？

column1: The glow of the city lights obliterate the stars.
column2: 城市燈火的光芒，把星星全都蓋過去了。

column1: As you bend over to do so, his bare teeth convince you otherwise.
column2: 你正彎腰要撿，\n牠齜牙一咧，你立刻打消念頭。

column1: You shinny up the pole, read the sign, and come right back down.
column2: 你爬上竿子看了看標誌，\n又馬上溜下來。

column1: Who'd want him?
column2: 誰會要牠啊？

column1: There is a sign on the pole.
column2: 竿子上有塊標誌。

column1: Bet is $%v200.00         You have $%v90.00
Congratulations, you've broken the bank! This casino has a limit of $250.00 per person, and you've reached it. No more for you, Larry!
column2: 恭喜，你把莊家壓垮了！\n這家賭場每人限贏 $250.00，\n你已經封頂啦。沒你的份了，賴瑞！
